metadata.title: Merge Two 2D Arrays by Summing Values

learning.whatWillYouLearn: Prerequisites: Arrays, Two Pointers, Loops, Sorting, Time Complexity
learning.whyDoesThisExist: Imagine two stores maintaining inventories where each product has a unique ID and quantity. When combining inventories, quantities for matching product IDs are added together while preserving sorted order.
learning.realWorldAnalogy: Imagine two stores maintaining inventories where each product has a unique ID and quantity. When combining inventories, quantities for matching product IDs are added together while preserving sorted order.
learning.whenToUseThis: Use the Two Pointers pattern whenever two sorted arrays need to be merged while combining matching elements.
learning.signals: Whenever two sorted arrays need to be merged while combining matching keys, think of the Two Pointers merge technique.
learning.expectedLearningOutcome: I will know when to use this pattern.

problem.problemStatement: You are given two 2D integer arrays nums1 and nums2. Each array consists of pairs [id, value] sorted in strictly increasing order by id. Merge the two arrays into one sorted array. If an id appears in both arrays, sum their values. Otherwise, keep the existing pair.

examples.explanation: IDs 1 and 4 appear in both arrays, so their values are summed.
No IDs overlap, so both arrays are simply merged.

constraints: IDs are unique within each array.
edgeCases: Remaining elements after one array ends

thinkingProcess.naiveIdea: Store all IDs and values inside a HashMap, then sort the IDs before constructing the answer.
thinkingProcess.keyInsight: Since both arrays are already sorted by id, use two pointers. Compare the current IDs and move the pointer with the smaller ID. If both IDs are equal, add their values and move both pointers.
thinkingProcess.ahaMoment: Since both arrays are sorted by ID, they can be merged exactly like Merge Sorted Array.
thinkingProcess.patternRecognition: Whenever two sorted arrays need to be merged while combining matching keys, think of the Two Pointers merge technique.
thinkingProcess.whyItWorks: Each element is processed exactly once. Since both arrays are already sorted, no additional sorting is required.
thinkingProcess.howToRecognizeSimilar: Whenever two sorted arrays need to be merged while combining matching keys, think of the Two Pointers merge technique.
commonWrongPaths: Ignoring that arrays are already sorted.

hints: Append remaining elements after one array finishes.
commonMisconceptions: Ignoring that arrays are already sorted.

pattern.name: Two Pointers on Sorted Arrays
pattern.recognitionSignals: Whenever two sorted arrays need to be merged while combining matching keys, think of the Two Pointers merge technique.

approaches.title: HashMap Merge
approaches.idea: Store all IDs and values inside a HashMap, then sort the IDs before constructing the answer.
approaches.dryRun: Merge into a map, then sort.

approaches.title: HashMap + Sorting
approaches.idea: Use a HashMap to merge values, then sort the final entries by ID.

approaches.title: Two Pointers on Sorted Arrays
approaches.idea: Since both arrays are sorted by ID, traverse them simultaneously. If IDs match, add their values. Otherwise, append the smaller ID and advance its pointer. Finally, append the remaining elements.
approaches.intuition: Each element is processed exactly once. Since both arrays are already sorted, no additional sorting is required.
algorithm: Initialize two pointers i and j.
If IDs are equal, sum the values and move both pointers.
If nums1 ID is smaller, add nums1[i] and move i.
Otherwise, add nums2[j] and move j.

code.python:
def mergeArrays(nums1, nums2):
    i = j = 0
    result = []

    while i < len(nums1) and j < len(nums2):
        if nums1[i][0] == nums2[j][0]:
            result.append([nums1[i][0], nums1[i][1] + nums2[j][1]])
            i += 1
            j += 1
        elif nums1[i][0] < nums2[j][0]:
            result.append(nums1[i])
            i += 1
        else:
            result.append(nums2[j])
            j += 1

    result.extend(nums1[i:])
    result.extend(nums2[j:])

    return result

steps: Merge equal IDs by summing values.

interviewFollowUps: Can this be solved using a HashMap?
How would you merge three arrays?
Can the merge be done in-place?
interviewTips: Notice that arrays are already sorted.
Avoid using a HashMap unless necessary.
State O(m+n) time and O(m+n) output space.

cheatSheet.keyObservation: Since both arrays are sorted by ID, they can be merged exactly like Merge Sorted Array.
cheatSheet.algorithm: Two Pointers → Compare IDs → Sum if Equal → Append Remaining.

importantPoints: IDs are unique within each array.
commonMistakes: Ignoring that arrays are already sorted.
